zh.lifeSummary: 當Raas VII de Gavere van Liedekerke於1218年出生在比利時奧斯吉福拉安德倫加維時，他的父親Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE34歲，母親Sophia van Breda en Schooten32歲。他於1237年與Jeanne de Wavrin結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1291年1月1日在他的家鄉享年73歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Raas VII de Gavere van Liedekerke于1218年出生在比利时奧斯吉福拉安德倫加維时，他的父亲Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE34岁，母亲Sophia van Breda en Schooten32岁。他于1237年与Jeanne de Wavrin结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1291年1月1日在他的家乡享年73岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Gavere
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Francois, Michel.
Altered form of French   Lariviere  .

variants.name: Lariviere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Normand, Fernand, Marcel, Adelard, Andre, Gaston, Philibert, Aime, Alcide, Alphonse, Antoine.
French (Larivière):
habitational name from any of numerous places in various parts of France named Larivière or La Rivière, from Old French riviere ‘river, shore’.

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Raas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Italian (Sicily; Rasà and Ràsa): habitational name from any of several places called Razzà in Sicily and Calabria.
Italian (Veneto): habitational name from Rasa in Rovigo province, but in some cases possibly from dialect rasa ‘resin’, denoting a resin collector or seller.
Indian: name of unexplained etymology, borne traditionally by leather workers (Mochi sub-caste).

nameMeanings.name: VII
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Vivian   and   Vivien .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Raas VII de Gavere van Liedekerke was born in 1218, in Gavere, East Flanders, Belgium, his father, Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE, was 34 and his mother, Sophia van Breda en Schooten, was 32. He married Jeanne de Wavrin in 1237. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 1 January 1291, in his hometown, at the age of 73.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Raas VII de Gavere van Liedekerke est né en 1218, à Gavere, Flandre-Orientale, Belgique, son père, Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Sophia van Breda en Schooten, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Jeanne de Wavrin en 1237. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 1 janvier 1291, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 73 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Raas VII de Gavere van Liedekerke 1218 in Gavere, Ostflandern, Belgien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE 34 und seine Mutter Sophia van Breda en Schooten 32. Er hat 1237 Jeanne de Wavrin geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 1. Januar 1291 im Alter von 73 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Raas VII de Gavere van Liedekerke è nato il nel 1218 nel Gavere, Fiandra Orientale, Belgio, il suo padre, Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Sophia van Breda en Schooten, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Jeanne de Wavrin nel 1237. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 1 gennaio 1291, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 73 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Raas VII de Gavere van Liedekerkeが1218年にベルギーオオスト-vラアンデレンガバーで生まれた時，父Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVEは34歳，母Sophia van Breda en Schootenは32歳であった。1237年にJeanne de Wavrinと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1291年1月1日に故郷で73歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Raas VII de Gavere van Liedekerke(이)가 1218년 벨기에 스트-블라아더 게이버에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Sophia van Breda en Schooten(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1237년 Jeanne de Wavrin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1291년 1월 1일 그의 고향에서 73세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Raas VII de Gavere van Liedekerke nasceu em 1218, em Gavere, Flandres Oriental, Bélgica, seu pai, Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Sophia van Breda en Schooten, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Jeanne de Wavrin em 1237. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1 de janeiro de 1291, em sua cidade natal, com 73 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Raas VII de Gavere van Liedekerke родился в 1218 г. в местности гавир, уст-влаандирен, Бельгия, его отцу по имени Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Sophia van Breda en Schooten, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Jeanne de Wavrin в 1237 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 1 января 1291 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 73 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Raas VII de Gavere van Liedekerke nació en 1218, en Gavere, Flandes Oriental, Bélgica, su papá, Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Sophia van Breda en Schooten, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Jeanne de Wavrin en 1237. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 1 de enero de 1291, en su pueblo, a la edad de 73 años.
